all.en: A neighbor mentioned that the community choir was looking for new members.
all.zh: 鄰居提到社區合唱團正在招募新團員。

all.en: I had not sung in public since my high school graduation, ten years ago.
all.zh: 我從十年前高中畢業以來，就沒在公眾場合唱過歌。

all.en: Still, I gathered my courage and showed up at the church hall on Tuesday.
all.zh: 儘管如此，我還是鼓起勇氣，週二到教堂的禮堂報到。

all.en: The conductor was warm and asked me to sing a few simple scales.
all.zh: 指揮很親切，請我唱幾段簡單的音階。

all.en: To my surprise, she placed me in the alto section right away.
all.zh: 令我驚訝的是，她立刻把我分到女低音聲部。

all.en: The other members welcomed me with smiles and handed me a thick folder of music.
all.zh: 其他團員微笑著歡迎我，並遞給我一本厚厚的樂譜夾。

all.en: We were preparing several pieces for the upcoming neighborhood festival.
all.zh: 我們正在為即將來臨的社區音樂節練習好幾首曲子。

all.en: Some songs were in English, while others were in Mandarin and Hakka.
all.zh: 有些歌是英文的，也有國語和客家話的歌。

all.en: At first, I struggled to follow the harmonies and lost my place often.
all.zh: 一開始我很難跟上和聲，常常唱錯地方。

all.en: After a month of practice, however, my confidence grew significantly.
all.zh: 不過經過一個月的練習，我的信心明顯提升。

all.en: On the night of the concert, our voices blended together beautifully.
all.zh: 音樂會當晚，我們的歌聲完美地融合在一起。

all.en: I left the stage feeling truly part of something bigger than myself.
all.zh: 走下舞台時，我覺得自己真正屬於一個比我更大的存在。
